codeReview.summary: The README section covers the requested headings, but two of the operational steps are materially inaccurate: it points readers to the full governed-system deployment command instead of the incremental market-version flow, and it documents borrower migration with the wrong callable shape while omitting the required coordinator permissions.

issues.description: Step 1 tells operators to run `npm run deploy:governed:testnet` to create a new market version under the existing factory/registry flow, but that script deploys a brand-new governed system. `scripts/deployGoverned.ts` calls `deployGovernedSystem(...)` and requires fresh `EMERGENCY_ADMIN`, `RISK_ADMIN`, and `TREASURY_OPERATOR` env vars, while `lib/deployment/deploySystem.ts:73-84` shows it creates a new AccessManager, WPAS, USDC, and then a market version. That does not match the documented goal of deploying an additional market and registering it in the existing `MarketVersionRegistry`.

The migration instructions are not executable as written. `MarketMigrationCoordinator` exposes `migrateBorrower(uint256 fromVersionId, uint256 toVersionId)`, not `migrateBorrower(account)` (`dualvm/contracts/migration/MarketMigrationCoordinator.sol:61`). In addition, the coordinator must be granted permission to call the restricted `exportPositionForMigration` / `importMigratedPosition` functions before migration works (`dualvm/test/MarketMigrationCoordinator.ts:29-32,42-45`; `dualvm/scripts/liveMigrationProof.ts:236-262`). The README currently implies that opening the route is sufficient and gives the wrong call shape.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `docs-worker` is out of sync with mission guidance for docs-only changes. The skill still says to run `npm test && npx tsc --noEmit` (and even build for CI edits), but mission `AGENTS.md` explicitly says not to rerun the full suite for docs-only work. The worker followed the mission rule and only ran lint, yet `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` remained true. The skill should be updated so future docs workers do not receive conflicting validation instructions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/docs-worker/SKILL.md:47,55 vs /home/kpa/.factory/missions/dc664f26-9086-4d9b-896d-ba8f3bde8d71/AGENTS.md:103-105; transcript skeleton at worker-transcripts.jsonl:81 shows lint-only validation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library notes the existence of `MarketMigrationCoordinator`, but it does not capture two operational constraints that matter for documentation accuracy: borrower/liquidity migration only works for the same collateral/debt asset pair, and the coordinator needs AccessManager wiring/privileges to call the restricted export/import functions on lending cores. Capturing those constraints in `.factory/library` would reduce future doc drift.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:22-23 only records `migrateBorrower() / migrateLiquidity()`, while `dualvm/contracts/migration/MarketMigrationCoordinator.sol:61-107` enforces route checks and same-asset-pair migration, and `dualvm/scripts/liveMigrationProof.ts:236-262` shows the extra governance grant needed for the coordinator.

summary: Reviewed the `market-factory-docs` feature handoff, transcript skeleton, diff, and supporting source files. The README addition does not accurately describe the live market-version deployment and migration flows, so the feature should be marked failed until the deployment command and migration instructions are corrected.
